CONTENT.Early life: Haugen hails from Verdalen. He started his career in Voll IL, but joined the youth ranks of Viking in 2007. It was a successful youth team, which won the national boys-16 football cup in 2009.
CONTENT.Club career: Haugen made his senior team debut in the 2012 La Manga Cup against FK Ekranas in February 2012, scoring his first first-team goal in the first round of the 2013 Norwegian Football Cup. He made his league debut when he started, and played 90 minutes, in the two last fixtures of the 2013 Tippeligaen.On 10 January 2018, Haugen signed a three-year contract with Molde FK. He made his debut for the club in the 5–0 home win against Sandefjord on 11 March 2018.On 15 June 2020, Haugen signed a new contract with Molde, until the end of the 2022 season.
CONTENT.International career: Haugen played a total of 25 games for Norway at international youth level.

SUMMARY: Kristoffer Haugen (born 21 February 1994) is a Norwegian professional footballer who plays as a left-back for Eliteserien club Molde.
